papers.title: LLaMA 3: Open Foundation Language Models
papers.summary: Open-weight LLM family (8B to 70B, later 405B) trained on 15T tokens with strong instruction-tuned variants. Permissive license suitable for commercial use.

insurance_business_case.headline: The credible 'sovereign AI' option for insurers in jurisdictions where data cannot leave the country or a specific cloud.
points: Host on-prem or in-region for Singapore / Hong Kong / Vietnam / Indonesia group-business books where PDPA / similar laws require it.
Removes per-token API costs for high-volume internal use cases (call summarization, knowledge base Q&A).
Fine-tunable on internal underwriting / claims data without exposing it to a third-party trainer.

technical_details.pattern: Self-hosted open-weight LLM platform with LoRA fine-tuning and vLLM serving.
points: Deploy on AKS / EKS GPU node pools (A100/H100); vLLM for high-throughput batched serving.
LoRA / QLoRA fine-tune on PII-scrubbed historical data; evaluate against same gold set used for closed models.
Model registry + signed weights; reproducible builds for SR 11-7 / OSFI E-23 model risk management.
Cost ceiling vs frontier API: typically break-even at ~10–50M tokens/day depending on tier.

papers.title: Segment Anything (SAM)
papers.summary: Foundation segmentation model that can isolate any object in any image from minimal prompts — trained on 1B masks.

insurance_business_case.headline: Backbone for the next generation of property, auto, and medical-imaging claim assessment.
points: Property claims: isolate roof, gutter, wall damage from a photo; estimate area and severity.
Auto (where carriers offer it): isolate panel-level damage from a few smartphone photos.
Medical imaging (Group LTD/Critical Illness): pre-segment radiology images for clinician reviewers.

technical_details.pattern: SAM as a callable computer-vision microservice, with downstream regressors for cost estimation.
points: Containerize SAM behind a REST/gRPC service with autoscaling on GPU pools.
Stack: SAM (segment) → classifier (damage type) → regressor (severity / area) → repair-cost lookup.
Human review queue keyed on (confidence, estimate, prior fraud signal).
Validate on held-out adjuster-labelled set; track per-class IoU and cost-estimate MAE.

papers.title: ESMFold: Evolutionary-scale Prediction of Atomic-level Protein Structure
papers.summary: Protein-language model that predicts atomic-resolution protein structures from sequence alone, faster (though slightly less accurate) than AlphaFold.

insurance_business_case.headline: Long-horizon disruption to mortality and morbidity assumptions underpinning life and health pricing.
points: Faster drug discovery shortens time-to-market for therapies that materially change mortality (oncology, ALS, rare disease).
Drives need for actuaries to model rapid morbidity-improvement scenarios in pricing & reserving.
Opportunity: partner with biotech-aligned health programs for differentiated wellness products.

technical_details.pattern: Actuarial scenario engine fed by biomedical-AI signal, not a direct production model.
points: Build a 'biomedical-AI signal' feed: tracked candidate drugs, trial phase, AI-predicted novel targets.
Translate into morbidity-improvement scenarios in the actuarial model (deterministic + stochastic).
Feed into IFRS 17 sensitivity disclosure and ORSA scenario library.
Cross-functional: Actuarial × Underwriting × Medical Directors × Strategy.

papers.title: Voicebox: Text-Guided Multilingual Universal Speech Generation at Scale
papers.summary: Non-autoregressive speech-generation model that can synthesize, edit, and style-transfer speech in multiple languages from text.

insurance_business_case.headline: Cost-out for omni-channel customer comms and a foundational risk for voice-channel identity assurance.
points: IVR / outbound voice in 20+ languages without per-language voice-actor cost.
Personalized renewal / claims-update voice messages at scale.
Threat: voice-cloning attacks on the phone channel (especially beneficiary changes, address changes).

technical_details.pattern: Generative speech for outbound + voice-biometric and challenge-response controls for inbound.
points: Outbound: TTS API + brand-voice + consent + DNC checks in a content pipeline.
Inbound: voice biometric enrolment at policy issue; verify on every authenticated phone call.
Risk-based step-up: high-impact requests (beneficiary, banking) require additional channel (push, OTP).
Detector model for synthetic-voice signal; capture and store waveform for forensics.

papers.title: Llama Guard: LLM-based Input-Output Safeguard for Human-AI Conversations
papers.summary: Open-weight 7B classifier specifically trained to label user / model messages against a customizable taxonomy of unsafe content.

insurance_business_case.headline: Free, self-hostable guardrail layer that meets the 'human-rights-of-data-subjects' obligations for AI-powered customer channels.
points: Filters PHI / PII oversharing before it reaches the LLM or downstream logs.
Catches mis-selling, prohibited financial advice, off-label medical claims in producer copilots.
Catches abuse / self-harm signals routed to a duty-of-care escalation path.

technical_details.pattern: Pre- and post-filter classifier on every LLM turn, with a domain-specific taxonomy.
points: Taxonomy as YAML: categories, severity, allowed/blocked actions, escalation route.
Inference: Llama Guard on cheap CPU/GPU pool; sub-100ms target.
Post-filter regenerates / redacts; pre-filter blocks or sanitizes the prompt.
Quarterly recalibration on red-team transcripts + customer complaint sample.
